_comment: Pre-built ammo categorization. Match key is 'pluginName.esm:EditorID' (case-insensitive). EditorID-only fallback also matched if the pluginName entry is not found. caliber: excluded|pistol|shotgun|rifle|large_rifle. damageType: ballistic|energy.

ammo._section: ==== Contraptions / Vault-Tec / Wasteland Workshop fireworks (DLCworkshop02.esm etc.) ====
==== Munitions - An Ammo Expansion (Munitions - An Ammo Expansion.esl) ====

ammo._comment: EditorIDs verified by parsing the .esl directly. Damage values + ballistic vs energy chosen to fit real-world caliber roles.
EditorIDs verified by parsing Munitions-Additional Ammo.esp directly (MAA_ prefix).

EditorIDs verified by parsing NewCalibers.esp directly. Note: NewCalibers re-uses some vanilla AMMO FormIDs (Ammo10mm, Ammo308Caliber, Ammo556, etc.) so the entries here are also valid for the vanilla forms when this plugin is loaded.
NewCalibers Ammo38Caliber is mis-named -- DATA shows it's actually .32 Auto Round (FULL = .32 Auto). Treating as pistol.
EditorIDs verified by parsing CALIBER-COMPLEX.esm directly. CC uses hyphenated names like 'Ammo-9X19MM-PARABELLUM' rather than the more common 'CC_Ammo' prefix used by other CC variants.

EditorIDs verified by parsing Armament.esp directly. Armament renames many vanilla AMMO records (same FormID, new EditorID via override) and also adds entirely new calibers. Records below are keyed by Armament.esp as source plugin. For vanilla records that Armament overrides (Ammo_10mm, Ammo_45ACP, etc.), GetFile(0) returns the originating plugin (Fallout4.esm/DLC), so we include both plugin-qualified AND EditorID-only entries for reliable matching.
--- Vanilla record overrides (Armament renames the EditorID) ---
--- DLC override (7.62x39mm from Nuka-World handmade round) ---

heuristics._comment: Pattern hints used by AmmoClassifier when an ammo isn't matched above. EditorID is checked case-insensitively for any of these substrings. Order: exclude_substrings first, then projectile-flag check, then energy_substrings (sets damageType only), then large_rifle > shotgun > rifle > pistol substrings. The projectile-flag check uses the ammo's BGSProjectile.flags: only Lobber/Flame/Cone/Barrier projectile types are auto-excluded plus non-hitscan projectiles that carry an attached explosion (real missiles/fatman). Hitscan Missile-type bullets (i.e. all regular guns) are NOT excluded.
heuristics._exclude_comment: If an EditorID matches ANY of these substrings (case-insensitive), the ammo is auto-excluded. Designed to catch things that should never count as a regular bullet: missiles, mininukes, grenades, flamer fuel, special creature attacks, traps, decorative fireworks, etc. Real bullet-firing weapons in vanilla and major overhauls do not contain these substrings, so this is safe.
